summary: Task 089 is complete in substance. The repo documents `SITE_URL` as a bare canonical public origin for both direct Node and Docker/Compose operators, includes restart and verification steps, and explicitly tells operators to submit `/sitemap.xml` after configuration without claiming provider-specific API integration. The implementation fails closed when `SITE_URL` is missing or invalid, emits canonical metadata only when configured, keeps published links homepage-only in the sitemap, and records homepage `lastmod` changes for public mutations. Unit, E2E, and smoke coverage all protect the discovery contract, and the required commands are recorded as passing.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Self-host and Docker guidance explain canonical-origin configuration and `/sitemap.xml` submission via `README.md`, `.env.example`, `.env.compose.example`, and aligned architecture/security/reliability docs.

Regression coverage protects robots behavior, sitemap contents, canonical metadata, missing/invalid-origin fail-closed behavior, and published-link exclusion through `tests/unit/public-site-discovery.test.ts`, `tests/unit/public-site-metadata.test.ts`, `tests/e2e/seo-discovery.spec.ts`, and `scripts/start-smoke.mjs`.
The shipped feature remains generic search-engine discovery only; docs consistently limit scope to sitemap, robots, and canonicals and explicitly exclude verification-file/DNS/API automation.
Required commands are already evidenced as passing: `npm run test:e2e -- --grep @seo-discovery`, `npm run verify`, and `npm run start:smoke`.
